# Habitat Skills — AI Agent Skills for Founders

A collection of AI agent skills that help aspiring founders go from idea to launched product. Built for [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) and compatible AI coding agents.

From the team behind [Habitat](https://joinhabitat.eu) — where 101+ founders have launched prototypes in a single evening.

## What are skills?

Skills are markdown files that give AI agents specialized knowledge. Install them in your project, and your agent knows how to validate ideas, research competitors, scope MVPs, and build landing pages — using the same frameworks experienced founders use.

## Available Skills

### Phase 1: Ideation & Validation

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`founder-context`](skills/founder-context/SKILL.md) | Set up your project context so all other skills know your idea, audience, and stage |
| [`idea-validation`](skills/idea-validation/SKILL.md) | Validate your idea with structured frameworks — TAM, problem-solution fit, Mom Test |
| [`competitor-research`](skills/competitor-research/SKILL.md) | Research competitors, find gaps, and identify positioning angles |
| [`customer-segments`](skills/customer-segments/SKILL.md) | Generate customer segments with personas, pain points, and outreach per segment |

### Phase 2: Prototyping & Building

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`landing-page`](skills/landing-page/SKILL.md) | Build a conversion-focused landing page — copy, structure, and code |
| [`prototype-sprint`](skills/prototype-sprint/SKILL.md) | Plan and execute a focused prototype sprint — scope, prioritize, build |
| [`mvp-scope`](skills/mvp-scope/SKILL.md) | Cut your feature list down to a true MVP with a clear build checklist |

### Phase 3: Demo & Pitch

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`pitch-deck`](skills/pitch-deck/SKILL.md) | Create pitches — 30-second elevator pitch, demo scripts, objection prep |

### Phase 4: Launch & Traction

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`first-users`](skills/first-users/SKILL.md) | Find your first 10, then 100 users with a targeted outreach playbook |
| [`launch-plan`](skills/launch-plan/SKILL.md) | Create a phased launch strategy — pre-launch, launch day, post-launch |
| [`social-content`](skills/social-content/SKILL.md) | Create launch posts, build-in-public updates, and social media content |

## Installation

### Quick Install (Recommended)

```bash
# Clone into your project
git clone https://github.com/ycanerden/habitat-skills.git .claude/skills/habitat
```

### Manual Install

1. Download the `skills/` folder
2. Copy it to `.claude/skills/habitat/` in your project
3. Skills will be available automatically

### Git Submodule

## What are skills?

## Available Skills

### Phase 1: Ideation & Validation

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`founder-context`](skills/founder-context/SKILL.md) | Set up your project context so all other skills know your idea, audience, and stage |

### Phase 3: Demo & Pitch

| Skill | Description |

| [`social-content`](skills/social-content/SKILL.md) | Create launch posts, build-in-public updates, and social media content |

## Installation

### Quick Install (Recommended)

```bash
# Clone into your project
git clone https://github.com/ycanerden/habitat-skills.git .claude/skills/habitat
```

### Manual Install

1. Download the `skills/` folder
2. Copy it to `.claude/skills/habitat/` in your project
3. Skills will be available automatically

### Git Submodule

```bash
git submodule add https://github.com/ycanerden/habitat-skills.git .claude/skills/habitat
```

## Usage

Skills activate automatically when you ask relevant questions:

```
"Help me validate my startup idea"          → idea-validation
"Research my competitors"                    → competitor-research
"Build me a landing page"                   → landing-page
"What should my MVP include?"               → mvp-scope
"How do I find my first users?"             → first-users
```

Or invoke directly:

```
/idea-validation
/competitor-research
/landing-page
```

## Quick Start

1. Install the skills
2. Run `/founder-context` to set up your project context
3. Start building — try `/idea-validation` to test your idea

## Built by Habitat

These skills were born from 23+ prototypes launched at [Habitat](https://joinhabitat.eu) evenings — where founders go from idea to shipped product in 5 hours.

Want to build alongside 50+ others? [Join the next evening →](https://joinhabitat.eu)

## Contributing

We welcome contributions. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines.

### Adding a new skill

1. Create a folder in `skills/` with a `SKILL.md` file
2. Follow the format of existing skills (YAML frontmatter + markdown)
3. Keep files under 500 lines

import { getAllSkillDescriptions } from "./skills";

export function buildSystemPrompt(): string {
  return `You are the Cofounder AI for Habitat — a startup advisor embedded in every founder's workflow.

Habitat is where 100+ founders have launched prototypes in a single evening. You carry that same spirit: practical, honest, action-oriented.

## Your role

You are a thinking partner and executor. You help founders move from fuzzy ideas to concrete next steps using proven frameworks. You don't just give advice — you produce outputs: interview guides, outreach emails, GTM plans, landing page copy, pitch scripts.

## Your personality

- **Direct**: Get to the point. Skip preamble.
- **Honest**: If an idea has a flaw, say it. Founders need truth, not cheerleading.
- **Specific**: No generic advice. Every output is tailored to this founder's situation.
- **Action-oriented**: End every response with a clear next step.
- **Concise**: Long responses are only appropriate for detailed outputs (templates, plans, copy). For conversation, be brief.

## Context awareness

Before asking questions, check if the founder has already shared their context (idea, audience, stage). If they have, use it. Don't re-ask what you already know.

## Available skills and when to use them

You have deep expertise in the following frameworks. Apply them when relevant:

${getAllSkillDescriptions()}

## How to signal which skill you're using

When you apply a skill framework, start your response with a skill tag on its own line:
\`[skill: skill-name]\`

Example: if helping with a GTM strategy, begin with \`[skill: gtm-strategy]\`

This helps the founder know which framework you're drawing from.

## Output quality

- For templates, emails, and copy: produce complete, ready-to-use output. No placeholders like "[insert pain point]" — fill them in based on what you know about the founder's situation.
- For plans and strategies: use clear sections, checklists, and concrete metrics.
- For conversation and advice: 2–4 sentences max unless the founder asks for more.

## What you don't do

- Don't ask more than 2 clarifying questions at once
- Don't give hypothetical advice without a concrete action attached
- Don't write generic content that could apply to any startup
- Don't use buzzwords (synergy, pivot, disrupt) unless being ironic
`;
}

Yes, Vercel is the right call for a Next.js app — zero config, free tier, deploys in 2 minutes.

Go to **vercel.com/new**, import `ycanerden/habitat-cofounder-ai`, and add one environment variable:

content: yeah but our api key is safe right so no one sees it?

message.content: yeah but our api key is safe right so no one sees it?

content.text:
Yes, completely safe. The API key lives in Vercel's environment variables (server-side only) and never touches the browser.

The flow is:

```
Browser → your Vercel server (/api/chat) → Anthropic API
                    ↑
            API key lives here only
```

The `/api/chat` route runs on the server — the key is never in the JavaScript that gets sent to users. Even if someone opens DevTools they won't find it.

The only risk would be if someone abuses your chat endpoint by spamming it (running up your Anthropic bill). If that becomes a concern later, you can add rate limiting or auth — but for now you're fine.
